blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: I'll try again.
Even after Vincent withdrew, Shirone continued to observe the notch in the tree for a long time without moving.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Currently, he was feeling a slight thrill.
Holding a line of technique in one's head is knowledge. But accepting it with the entire body is sensation.
Strike the same spot, but twist it slightly.
This simple knowledge, passed down through the mouths of many people, was being absorbed by Shirone as a much greater realization than Vincent had expected.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Dad.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Yes?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Should I try to break this in one go?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Hahaha! You haven't even chopped halfway, how could it break?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If I'm lucky, it might happen.
No tree breaks because of luck. Skilled lumberjacks relentlessly exploit the weakness of the grain and topple them with just a few axe strokes, but Shirone couldn't possibly do that.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Alright! Let's believe in our son's luck!
Vincent heartily played along with his son. He was just grateful that he showed interest in wood chopping.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If I break this, grant me one wish.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Hm? A wish?
A sudden unease crept in. What if he wants to learn writing? Or asks to be sent to school like children from rich families?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: When you go to the city to sell things next time, take me with you.
To be honest, Vincent was immensely relieved, but he didn't show it and laughed boisterously.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Haha, if that's all, sure. I'll grant it!
Having received permission, Shirone took the axe behind his shoulder and looked at the tree.
Soon, as the smile on his lips disappeared, Vincent felt a chill.
His son, not even blinking, was staring at the tree as if searching for something invisible.
Shirone suddenly swung the axe.
It seemed to hit the notch precisely, but mixed in at an extremely subtle level, imperceptible to human recognition, was a slight twist.
Crackboom!
Vincent opened his eyes wide at the sound like lightning striking.
Cracks like an earthquake occurred at the spot where the axe entered, and the tree broke with a loud creak, unable to bear its weight.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hooray! I did it!

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Vincent couldn't believe it.
It was the 'Thunder Chopping' passed down as a legend among lumberjacks.
Neither Vincent nor any lumberjack he knew had ever seen or experienced this mythical technique.
But that's because lumberjacks' work is focused on livelihood.
In reality, there is one profession where the Thunder Chopping phenomenon can be encountered quite frequently, and that's swordsmanship.
Swordsmen, who wield their swords with their lives at stake, hone techniques that are incomparably more systematic and profound than lumberjacks.
Thunder Chopping was among these techniques.
But it was clear that even for such swordsmen, it wasn't an easy technique to master.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hooray! I did it!
Shirone, who would now be able to visit the city as promised, jumped up and down with both arms raised.
Vincent, watching him, was confused.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: He couldn't decide whether he should raise his son as a lumberjack or not.
Vincent held the reins of a twin-horse carriage with a luggage cart and passed through the gates of Creas City.
Shirone, sitting in the cargo area, looked around with sparkling eyes.
Leather goes to the weapons shop, meat to the grocery store, and entrails to the herb shop or magic shop.
They had to visit several stores, and if they included bargaining, it would take at least 4 hours.
Upon arriving at the store, Shirone jumped off the cart.
As Vincent placed a bag of meat on his shoulder, he said:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We need to return before sunset.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Don't worry. I've memorized all the roads.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Never go into obscure places, stick to the main roads. If someone comes and asks why you're alone, point to the nearest store and say you're waiting for your dad.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Okay. But nothing happened the last time I came, you know.
Vincent felt pained at the thought of leaving his son.
But the family's livelihood depended on how well he negotiated, so it couldn't be helped.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone reassured his worried father and left the shopping district.
Vincent expected that he would go to places children typically visit like performance halls or toy shops, but Shirone had no interest in such things.
The place Shirone arrived at was the largest library in the city.
Looking up at the vast, majestic building, his heart pounded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Could all the knowledge since the beginning of time be accumulated in this building?
There was no way to confirm.
The library, being a high-end cultural space, was inaccessible to anyone who wasn't a noble.
When two female students walked out of the library holding books, Shirone quickly stepped aside.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Vincent was an affectionate father, but he always threatened like an ogre when talking about nobles.
Never defy them. Their words are always right. If you make eye contact, bow your head.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone didn't care how great the nobles were.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He just wanted to read books.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why can't I enter?
Shirone decided to follow the female students.
Not only was there nowhere else he wanted to see, but he was also curious about how nobles differed from commoners.
After walking for about 20 minutes, the scenery changed dramatically.
Shirone, forgetting his original purpose, was overwhelmed by the enormous structures rising in all directions.
The crown jewel among them was a school that boasted a vast area even when seen through iron bars.
The building name was inscribed on the arch of the main gate.
Alpheus Magic School.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If there was one word that Shirone, in his life, couldn't understand, it was magic.
It appears consistently in numerous stories, but he had never seen a book that explained its principles.
It was a kind of disregard. It was also arrogance, suggesting that those who aren't magicians don't need to know.
A guard spotted Shirone and shouted:
What! What are you doing, kid?
And waved his hands back and forth as if shooing flies, seeing Shirone's attire was that of a commoner.
Get out of here! This isn't a place for vermin like you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, I'm sorry.
Shirone fled in fear.
His knees weakened when the burly-looking guard glared at him.
But when the guard was out of sight, he stopped walking again.
Surprisingly, he was still under the walls of the magic school.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How vast is this school anyway?
While thinking this, he heard an old man's voice from beyond the wall.
Now, let's talk about what magic is.
blocks.speaker: Alpheus

blocks.content: Ahh, no! Show us magic. Just one more!
blocks.speaker: Child

blocks.content: Fire! Show us the fire, Principal!
blocks.speaker: Child

blocks.content: Shirone looked up at the towering tree standing beyond the wall.
The principal of the magic school seemed to be conducting an outdoor class under the tree's shade.
The voices of the children were all younger than Shirone.
Since nobles are educated from the moment they're born, it wasn't strange for young children to attend magic school.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Hohoho, lighting a fire at school would get me in trouble.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Instead, if you answer the question correctly, I'll show you an interesting magic.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes! What's the question? Ask quickly!
Shirone put his ear to the wall.
What is the most necessary talent for learning magic?
Silence followed for a while.
Everyone must have been speechless.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even to Shirone, it was a question requiring quite a subjective answer.
But the children, recognized for their talent in magic and admitted early, were geniuses.
Rather than giving up, they each shared their own answers.

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: Effort. Since magic can't be fully learned even if studied for a lifetime, effort is important.

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: I think it's knowledge. I've read over 100 magic books.
Other plausible answers like concentration and memory followed.
But the principal's voice was still not heard.
Perhaps he was smiling kindly while watching the children?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Money. Because there's so much to buy to learn magic.
The children burst into laughter.
The principal's laughter was mixed in as well.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: By this point, Shirone was also curious.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If not effort, knowledge, or money, what is the most necessary talent for learning magic?
After hearing all the children's answers, the principal finally spoke.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: The most necessary talent for learning magic is insight.
A second silence fell.
What's insight?
The principal made a troubled groan.
But soon, in a kind voice, he continued his explanation.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Insight is something more accurate than knowledge and faster than effort.

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: Wow! Then it's totally like magic?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Hoho! It could be. No, that's right. All magic in the world stems from insight.
Let me give an example.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Do you know what 1 plus 1 is?

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: Of course it's 2.
The children answered in unison, as if asking how he could ask such a thing.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: I see. Then, can you explain why 1 plus 1 is 2?
Huh? Well, obviously...
The children, who were about to speak confidently, closed their mouths.
They couldn't grasp where to start the explanation or how to explain it.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: That strange feeling you're experiencing right now is insight.
Long ago, there was a time when people didn't know that 1 plus 1 is 2.
It was proven through knowledge and effort.
But you understand perfectly that 1 plus 1 is 2 without going through that process, don't you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone became increasingly engrossed in the story.
The children beyond the wall were probably also listening with their mouths agape.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Magic is a phenomenon that has always existed.
Just like how the answer is 2 even when people didn't know that 1 plus 1 is 2.
blocks.speaker: Alpheus

blocks.content: Someone uncovered that fact through effort and knowledge, but you don't need to do that.
blocks.speaker: Alpheus
Alpheus
blocks.content: Thus, insight is the fastest means to understand the rules of something.

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: So there's no need to study or make effort?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Is that what I'm saying?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: But to be honest... yes, that's the case.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Common sense is knowledge believed by the majority.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But sometimes, it's common sense because it's easy to accept.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone could guess Alpheus's feelings of having to tell the cruel reality.

blocks.speaker: Child
blocks.content: So we don't need to go to school?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Hohoho! Insight isn't something easily obtained.
The reason why 1 plus 1 is 2 was proven by scholars over a long period.
blocks.speaker: Alpheus

blocks.content: Of course, there are people who grasp the answer without going through the verification process.
blocks.speaker: Alpheus

blocks.content: We call such people geniuses.
blocks.speaker: Alpheus
Child
blocks.content: My mom said I'm a genius?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: That's not wrong either.
Every human is born with talent, and if they hone that talent, anyone can become a genius.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Anyone can become a genius.
Those words lodged deeply in Shirone's heart.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But is that really true?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Would the opportunity to cross this high wall come to him as well?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Yes.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: You, the child standing behind the wall.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: I'd like to hear your thoughts.
Shirone hastily retreated from the wall.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Should he run away?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, should he answer?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But does a commoner have the right to answer?

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: Don't do that, come over here.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: I want to see your face.
Shirone approached the wall, hearing the sound of his beating heart.
Though still young and unaware of the ways of the world, one thing was clear: if he didn't cross this wall now, he would regret it for the rest of his life.
Shirone climbed the wall.
Alpheus, the principal of Alpheus Magic School, was smiling.
The first impression was of a white-haired old grandfather.
But in reality, he was a well-respected figure, a certified level 4 magician recognized by the kingdom, known even in foreign countries.
Alpheus, sitting on a flat rock, waved his hand.
Come on over. Have you come to keep this old man company?
Gaining confidence from Alpheus's kindness, Shirone jumped over the wall.
As expected, children around Shirone's age or younger were sitting in a circle.
